canonical_question: How do I write recursive CTE queries for hierarchies (org charts, MLM, BOM)?
aliases: WITH RECURSIVE query for tree traversal
bill of materials recursive SQL query
org chart SQL query parent child

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: SQL:1999 introduced recursive CTEs; syntax stable since

constraints: MySQL requires WITH RECURSIVE keyword even for simple recursion
SQL Server limits recursion to 100 levels by default (MAXRECURSION)
Never use UNION (dedup) in recursive CTEs on large hierarchies without understanding the sort cost
Recursive member must not contain aggregate functions, GROUP BY, window functions in MySQL
Always implement cycle detection or depth limits for cyclic data

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to traverse a DAG or general graph with multiple parents per node
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use closure table pattern or graph database (Neo4j, Apache AGE)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Hierarchy has millions of nodes and read performance is critical
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Consider nested set or materialized path models for read-heavy workloads

inputs_needed.question: Which database engine are you using?
What kind of hierarchy are you modeling?
Is your workload primarily reads or writes?
